questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Patents are granted for inventions that have a practical application.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Patent law requires that the invention be new, involve an inventive step, and be capable of industrial application.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because patent law requires that the invention meet the criteria of novelty, non-obviousness, and utility.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Copyright laws protect original literary, dramatic, musical, and artistic works.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Copyright laws grant the creator of the work exclusive rights to reproduce, distribute, and display the work.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because copyright laws protect original works and grant the creator exclusive rights to control the work.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Trademarks are granted for distinctive signs or symbols that identify a business or product.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Trademark law requires that the sign or symbol be capable of distinguishing the goods or services of one business from those of another.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because trademark law requires that the sign or symbol be distinctive and capable of distinguishing the goods or services of one business from those of another.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Industrial designs are protected under the Designs Act.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The Designs Act grants exclusive rights to the owner of an industrial design to produce, sell, or import the design.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the Designs Act grants exclusive rights to the owner of an industrial design.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Geographical indications are protected under the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This Act grants protection to geographical indications that are used to identify the geographical origin of goods.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act grants protection to geographical indications that identify the geographical origin of goods.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Copyright protection is not available for ideas, procedures, methods of operation, or mathematical concepts.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Copyright protects only original literary, dramatic, musical, and artistic works, not ideas or concepts.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because copyright protects only original works, not ideas or concepts.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Trademarks can be registered in any color.
questions.reason: Reason (R): However, it is recommended to use a single color to avoid any potential disputes.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because trademarks can be registered in any color, but it is recommended to use a single color to avoid disputes.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Industrial designs can be protected through registration or unregistered design right.
questions.reason: Reason (R): However, unregistered design right is available only for certain types of designs.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A. Industrial designs can be protected through registration or unregistered design right, but unregistered design right is available only for certain types of designs.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Geographical indications are protected only for agricultural products.
questions.reason: Reason (R): However, geographical indications can be used to identify the geographical origin of any type of good.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because A is false, but R is true. Geographical indications are not limited to agricultural products and can be used to identify the geographical origin of any type of good.

questions.reason: Reason (R): Copyright protection is granted as soon as the work is created.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because A is false, but R is true. Copyright protection is not automatic, but it is granted as soon as the work is created.
